steps.title: Match the singular subject
steps.content: The subject is it, referring to the tamarack tree. The sentence describes a regular present-tense action, so the verb needs the third-person singular present form before all its needles.

steps.title: Reject nonfinite or mismatched forms
steps.content:
• Choice A is the base form and does not agree with the singular subject.
  • Choice B uses have, which does not agree with singular it.
  • Choice D is a participle and cannot serve as the sentence's finite main verb by itself.
The answer is C.
